message: Proposed root change telemetry-persistence as a thin vertical slice of the persistence layer (no HTTP routes — those are the dependent endpoint changes). Wrote 2 feature files: persist-telemetry-event.feature (atomic raw_events append + vehicle_current_state upsert via INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE, env-based connection config) and concurrent-upsert-and-aggregate.feature (GROUP BY aggregate over vehicle_current_state, last-event-wins, 50-vehicle concurrency with no lost/double-counted upserts, counts sum to distinct vehicles). Wrote plan.md with Why/What Changes/Design/Tasks covering versioned migrations for raw_events + vehicle_current_state, persist_telemetry() and aggregate_fleet_state(), docker-compose.test.yml Postgres, and integration tests. Aligned with telemetry-architecture (DB-enforced concurrency, derived aggregate not materialized counter) and tech-stack (Python 3.14/uv/Postgres) standards. ratchet status: 2/2 artifacts complete. AI build log written to docs/ai-build-logs/ and index updated. No implementation performed.

Starting APPLY: read plan/features/standards. Scaffolding uv+Postgres persistence layer (migrations, persist_telemetry, aggregate_fleet_state) + docker-compose.test.yml + integration tests. Installing docker compose plugin (missing locally) to run proof-of-work.

Implemented the telemetry-persistence layer (all plan tasks 1.1-5.1). Added uv project (pyproject.toml, uv.lock, requires-python >=3.14; psycopg, psycopg-pool, pydantic, fastapi); env-only DSN in app/config.py (DATABASE_URL, no hard-coded creds); pooled connections in app/db.py; versioned migrations app/migrations/0001_create_raw_events.sql (append-only, indexed) and 0002_create_vehicle_current_state.sql (PK vehicle_id, status CHECK idle|moving|charging|fault) applied by app/migrate.py. app/persistence.py: persist_telemetry() writes raw append + INSERT...ON CONFLICT (vehicle_id) DO UPDATE in one transaction (atomic, no read-then-write); aggregate_fleet_state() is a single GROUP BY status, zero-filled. Added docker-compose.test.yml (Postgres 16) + Dockerfile + 4 integration tests covering first/later upsert, atomic rollback, 50 distinct vehicles concurrent (counts sum to 50, last-event-wins), and repeated single-vehicle upserts counting once. Aligned with telemetry-architecture (DB-enforced concurrency, no materialized counter, no HTTP/Redis/CDC) and tech-stack. Proof-of-work: docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm api pytest -q tests/integration -> exit 0, 6 passed. (Installed Homebrew docker-compose and linked it as a docker compose CLI plugin since the plugin was missing locally.)

Added the stateless ingestion API's POST /telemetry route. app/ingestion_api.py defines a dedicated FastAPI app (separate from the future frontend API): the body is validated into the existing TelemetryEvent (Pydantic extra='forbid'), schema-invalid bodies are rejected 422 before the handler runs, valid events call the existing atomic persist_telemetry and return 201 Created — validate->write->return, no broker publish. Added httpx>=0.27 dev dep (re-locked uv.lock) and tests/integration/test_ingest_post.py exercising it in-process via TestClient against real Postgres (valid->201 with one raw + one current row; later same-vehicle POST upserts single current row + appends raw; 7 invalid-body cases->422 with nothing persisted). Proof-of-work: docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm api pytest tests/integration/test_ingest_post.py -> exit 0, 9 passed; full integration suite 15/15. Plan tasks 1.1-4.1 checked off; AI build log written and index updated.

Proposed change 'fleet-state-endpoint' (read half of the ingest-and-fleet-state phase). Created .ratchet/changes/fleet-state-endpoint/ with features/fleet-state-endpoint/get-fleet-state.feature (5 scenarios: per-status counts, zero-fill, latest-event-per-vehicle, sum-to-distinct-vehicles, concurrency consistency, read/write separation) and plan.md with a 6-task checklist. Thin vertical slice: add a separate frontend FastAPI app (app/frontend_api.py) exposing GET /fleet/state that reuses the existing aggregate_fleet_state() GROUP BY read and returns zero-filled per-status JSON, plus the phase proof-of-work test tests/integration/test_ingest_fleet_state.py (50 concurrent vehicles -> counts sum to 50, match last event per vehicle). Honors telemetry-architecture standard (ingestion stays write-only/stateless; reads on a separate frontend API; concurrency in the DB). Documented scoped deviation: reads from the single primary since this phase has no replica yet. No tasks implemented in this step.

Implemented fleet-state-endpoint (apply): added app/frontend_api.py — a dedicated frontend FastAPI app, separate from the stateless ingestion API per the telemetry-architecture standard — exposing GET /fleet/state, which returns the existing aggregate_fleet_state() per-status counts (idle|moving|charging|fault, all keys present, zero-filled) as 200 JSON. No in-process counter; counts come from a single GROUP BY MVCC snapshot on the primary (scoped single-Postgres deviation). Added tests/integration/test_fleet_state_get.py (3.1 mixed statuses with zero-fill, 3.2 empty DB all-zero) and the phase proof-of-work tests/integration/test_ingest_fleet_state.py (3.3): concurrently POSTs 50 distinct vehicles across mixed statuses via the ingestion app, then GET /fleet/state counts sum to 50 and exactly match the last event per vehicle. Wrote the AI build-log and appended the index line. Proof-of-work passes exit 0; full integration suite 18/18. All plan tasks 1.1-4.1 checked off.

Proposed change 'zone-counts-increment' (root of phase zone-traversal-counter). Wrote .ratchet/changes/zone-counts-increment/ with two Gherkin feature files — seed-zone-counts.feature (migration creates zone_counts PK zone_id/entry_count; seed ~20-zone hardcoded constant idempotently; reads always report all zones zero-filled) and atomic-zone-increment.feature (non-null zone_entered triggers single server-side 'UPDATE zone_counts SET entry_count = entry_count + 1 WHERE zone_id = $1' inside the existing ingest transaction; null leaves counts unchanged; N concurrent entries to one zone yield exactly N with no lost updates; forbidden SELECT-then-UPDATE not used) — plus plan.md with Why/What/Design and a ## Tasks checklist of 11 unchecked items. Vertical slice: schema + atomic increment in persist_telemetry + zone_entry_counts() read seam, proven by tests/integration/test_zone_increment.py. HTTP GET /zones/counts and the phase proof test_zone_counts.py are scoped to the follow-on zone-counts-endpoint change. No implementation done in this step.

Implemented zone-counts-increment (all plan tasks 1.1-4.1). Migration 0003 creates zone_counts(zone_id PK, entry_count). Added ZONES (~20 hardcoded zones) and optional zone_entered to TelemetryEvent. seed_zones() idempotently seeds one row per zone (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) at the end of run_migrations. persist_telemetry now runs a single server-side atomic UPDATE zone_counts SET entry_count = entry_count + 1 WHERE zone_id = %(zone_id)s inside the existing transaction when zone_entered is non-null (no SELECT-then-UPDATE); no counter statement when null. Added zone_entry_counts() read seam. 4 new integration tests in test_zone_increment.py cover exact-zone increment, null leaves all at 0, 50 concurrent entries -> entry_count==50 with zero lost updates, and all ~20 seeded zones returned with live totals. Rebuilt api image; full integration suite passes 22/22, exit 0. AI build log + index updated.

Proposing root change 'anomaly-detection': anomalies table + synchronous stateless/stateful detection inside persist_telemetry + indexed read seam. HTTP GET /anomalies and the by-absence comms-loss watchdog are deferred to the follow-on changes (anomaly-query-endpoint, comms-loss-watchdog), mirroring the zone-counts-increment slice boundary.

Created the 'anomaly-detection' root change for phase 3 (vertical slice, no implementation). Artifacts: two Gherkin feature files — detect-anomalies-in-ingest.feature (stateless rules: fault_status, error_codes, low_battery<15 while not charging, overspeed>5; stateful rules vs the prior persisted reading: stuck moving<0.1 for >=10s, teleport implied >15 m/s, battery_rising while not charging; strict thresholds, no firing on first reading, all written in-transaction) and query-recent-anomalies.feature (indexed read seam filtered by vehicle_id + inclusive [since, until]). plan.md scopes the thinnest end-to-end slice: migrations 0004 anomalies table (+ (vehicle_id, detected_at) index) and 0005 extend vehicle_current_state with speed_mps/pos_x/pos_y; extend TelemetryEvent (speed_mps, error_codes, pos_x/pos_y) + threshold constants; synchronous detection inside persist_telemetry's existing transaction; recent_anomalies(vehicle_id, since, until) read seam; persistence-level integration test tests/integration/test_anomaly_detection.py — with a 14-item '## Tasks' checklist. Deliberately deferred (mirroring the zone-counts-increment slice boundary): GET /anomalies (anomaly-query-endpoint), the by-absence comms-loss watchdog (comms-loss-watchdog), and the full phase proof tests/integration/test_anomalies.py.

Implemented synchronous in-transaction anomaly detection + read seam (plan tasks 1.1-6.1). Added migration 0004 (anomalies table + composite (vehicle_id, detected_at) index) and 0005 (speed_mps/pos_x/pos_y on vehicle_current_state). Extended TelemetryEvent with speed_mps/error_codes/pos_x/pos_y and added default-threshold constants. persist_telemetry now reads the prior current-state row before the upsert, evaluates stateless rules (fault_status, error_codes, low_battery<15 while not charging, overspeed>5; strict comparisons) and stateful rules vs prior (stuck: both moving & speed<0.1 & dt>=10s; teleport: euclidean dist/dt>15 m/s; battery_rising while not charging; none fire without a prior row), and INSERTs one anomalies row per fired rule with detected_at=recorded_at — all inside the existing transaction. Added recent_anomalies(vehicle_id, since, until) indexed inclusive-bounds read seam. New integration test test_anomaly_detection.py (19 tests) covers every rule's fire/no-fire boundary, first-reading suppression, multi-violation one-row-per-rule, and the windowed/vehicle-filtered read. conftest truncates anomalies; helper anomaly_types added. Slice test 19/19 and full suite 46/46 pass (exit 0) against real Postgres after rebuilding the api image. AI build-log written + index updated. GET /anomalies endpoint and comms-loss watchdog remain follow-on changes (out of scope).

Added GET /anomalies to the frontend API (app/frontend_api.py) as a thin adapter over the existing recent_anomalies(vehicle_id, since, until) read seam: required vehicle_id + inclusive [since, until] ISO-8601 timestamp query params, returns 200 with the matching anomalies (vehicle_id, anomaly_type, detail, detected_at) as a JSON list ordered by detected_at, empty list when none — an indexed range scan over (vehicle_id, detected_at), no in-process state. Landed the phase proof-of-work tests/integration/test_anomalies.py: each default anomaly class fires exactly when its threshold is crossed and not otherwise, driven through the ingestion POST /telemetry write path and read back via GET /anomalies (fault_status, error_codes, low_battery, overspeed, stuck, teleport, battery_rising, clean event), end-to-end HTTP reachability, and window inclusion/exclusion with inclusive bounds + vehicle filtering. Comms-loss-gap scenario + by-absence watchdog remain the out-of-scope comms-loss-watchdog follow-on. Plan tasks 1.1-4.1 checked off; build log + index updated.
